description:
You are a security engineer who must plan the first-day roll-out of a quarterly
    AWS access-key rotation.  
    1.  A CSV extract of every currently active key is already present at  
        /home/user/resources/credentials_2024Q2.csv  
        and has the exact four-column format shown below (including the header line):
            user_id,access_key,created,expires
            …other data rows…
        Dates use the ISO pattern YYYY-MM-DD.
    2.  The policy is:  
        • Rotate **no more than four (4)** keys per day.  
        • On each day choose the four keys whose *expires* date is the soonest.  
          (If two keys share the same date, their relative order does not matter.)  
        • Day-1 rotations must therefore be exactly the four keys with the earliest
          expiry dates in the file.
    3.  To show you can automate the decision rather than hand-pick the rows,
        write a small optimisation model (for instance with the PuLP Python
        interface to GLPK) that minimises  
              “sum of days-to-expiry for all keys rotated today”  
        subject to the single constraint “rotate ≤ 4 keys”.
        • The model itself is not graded, but it must exist as the file  
          /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_ilp.py
          so that an auditor can review it later.  
        • The script must read /home/user/resources/credentials_2024Q2.csv and write
          its result (the set of four chosen rows) to the files described next.
    4.  When your script has solved the model, it must create the following two
        artefacts in the directory /home/user/cred_rotation (create the directory
        if it does not yet exist):

        a)  /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_day1.csv  
            EXACT required content and layout:
                Line-1 (header):   user_id,access_key
                Line-2-5:          the four chosen user_id / access_key pairs,
                                 one per line, comma-separated, **in ascending
                                 order of the expires date**.  
            The automated test will read this file and must find those five lines
            – nothing more, nothing less.

        b)  /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_audit.log  
            EXACT required content and layout (four lines total):  
                2024-04-01 09:00:00,<user_id>,<access_key>
            Replace <user_id> and <access_key> with the same values that
            appear in rotation_day1.csv.  
            Every line must repeat the static timestamp “2024-04-01 09:00:00”.
            The automated test will compare the entire file byte-for-byte.

    5.  Summary of what the grader will verify:
        • /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_ilp.py exists (content not inspected).  
        • /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_day1.csv exists and contains exactly
          the header plus the four earliest-expiry rows, comma-separated,
          no trailing spaces or blank lines.  
        • /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_audit.log exists and contains
          exactly four identical-timestamp lines matching the same
          user_id/access_key pairs found in rotation_day1.csv.

    IMPORTANT:  
    • Do NOT modify /home/user/resources/credentials_2024Q2.csv.  
    • Make sure the directory and both output files are world-readable
      (mode 0644 is fine).  
    • Use only commands available to an unprivileged user.  
    • You may `pip install --user pulp` (or a different solver) if you wish,
      but it is not mandatory as long as the output files are 100 % correct.  

    When you are finished, both output files must already exist on disk with the
    exact content and path described above; no further manual steps are allowed.
truth:
Initial system state created for the candidate:

    1.  The file that the agent must read:
        Path: /home/user/resources/credentials_2024Q2.csv  
        Mode: 0644  
        Content (7 data rows, one header row, LF line endings):
            user_id,access_key,created,expires
            u101,AKIA111111,2024-01-15,2024-06-10
            u102,AKIA222222,2024-02-10,2024-04-30
            u103,AKIA333333,2024-01-20,2024-05-15
            u104,AKIA444444,2024-03-01,2024-04-20
            u105,AKIA555555,2024-02-18,2024-07-01
            u106,AKIA666666,2024-02-25,2024-04-18
            u107,AKIA777777,2024-01-30,2024-04-25

    2.  Expected output after the agent completes the task:

        a) /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_day1.csv  
           Mode: 0644  
           Exact 5-line content (newline at end of the last line):
                user_id,access_key
                u106,AKIA666666
                u104,AKIA444444
                u107,AKIA777777
                u102,AKIA222222

        b) /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_audit.log  
           Mode: 0644  
           Exact 4-line content (newline at end of the last line):
                2024-04-01 09:00:00,u106,AKIA666666
                2024-04-01 09:00:00,u104,AKIA444444
                2024-04-01 09:00:00,u107,AKIA777777
                2024-04-01 09:00:00,u102,AKIA222222

        c) /home/user/cred_rotation/rotation_ilp.py  
           Mode: 0644  
           Content not inspected by the grader, only existence is required.

    3.  Directory /home/user/cred_rotation does not exist before the task starts
        and therefore must be created by the agent.

    The automated test suite will:
        • Verify the presence and exact content of the two files under
          /home/user/cred_rotation as shown above.
        • Fail the task if any extra whitespace, extra lines or different ordering
          (with respect to ascending expiry) is detected.
